0.original: Your mind is a thought factory.
0.b1: Your mind is like a factory that makes thoughts.
0.cn: 你的大脑就像一座生产思想的工厂。
0.ai_notes: 'thought factory': a metaphor describing the mind as a place where ideas are created.
0.remarks: 'thought factory': 一个比喻，将思维描述为产生想法的地方。

1.original: It's a busy factory producing countless thoughts in one day.
1.b1: It is a busy factory that makes many thoughts every day.
1.cn: 这是一座繁忙的工厂，每天都在生产无数的思想。
1.ai_notes: 'countless': a way to say 'very many' or 'too many to count'.
1.remarks: 'countless': 形容“非常多”或“多到数不清”。

2.original: Production in your thought factory is under the charge of two foremen, one of whom we will call Mr. Triumph and the other Mr. Defeat.
2.b1: Two managers run this factory: Mr. Triumph and Mr. Defeat.
2.cn: 工厂里有两位工头负责管理，一位叫“成功先生”，另一位叫“失败先生”。
2.ai_notes: 'under the charge of': responsible for managing or controlling something. 'foreman': a person in charge of a group of workers in a factory.
2.remarks:
'under the charge of': 负责管理或控制某事。
'foreman': 工厂里负责管理一群工人的领班。

3.original: Mr. Triumph is in charge of manufacturing positive thoughts.
3.b1: Mr. Triumph is responsible for making positive thoughts.
3.cn: 成功先生负责制造积极的思想。
3.ai_notes: 'in charge of': having control or responsibility for a task or group.
3.remarks: 'in charge of': 对某项任务或群体拥有控制权或责任。

4.original: He specializes in producing reasons why you can, why you're qualified, why you will.
4.b1: He focuses on reasons why you can do things and why you will succeed.
4.cn: 他专门负责寻找你能做到、你有资格、你一定会成功的理由。
4.ai_notes: 'specializes in': to focus on one specific area or skill. 'qualified': having the right skills or knowledge to do something.
4.remarks:
'specializes in': 专注于某个特定领域或技能。
'qualified': 具备做某事所需的技能或知识。

5.original: The other foreman, Mr. Defeat, produces negative, deprecating thoughts.
5.b1: The other manager, Mr. Defeat, makes negative and bad thoughts.
5.cn: 另一位工头，失败先生，负责制造消极和贬低自己的思想。
5.ai_notes: 'deprecating': expressing disapproval or low opinion of oneself or others.
5.remarks: 'deprecating': 表示对他人或自己持否定态度或低评价。

6.original: He is your expert in developing reasons why you can't, why you're weak, why you're inadequate.
6.b1: He is an expert at finding reasons why you cannot do things or why you are not good enough.
6.cn: 他是寻找你做不到、你很软弱、你不够优秀这些理由的专家。
6.ai_notes: 'inadequate': not good enough or not capable of doing a task.
6.remarks: 'inadequate': 不够好，或没有能力完成某项任务。

7.original: His specialty is the why-you-will-fail chain of thoughts.
7.b1: He specializes in thoughts about why you will fail.
7.cn: 他的专长是制造关于你为什么会失败的连锁想法。
7.ai_notes: 'chain of thoughts': a series of connected ideas that lead to a specific conclusion.
7.remarks: 'chain of thoughts': 一系列相互关联、导向特定结论的想法。

8.original: Both Mr. Triumph and Mr. Defeat are intensely obedient.
8.b1: Both Mr. Triumph and Mr. Defeat listen to you very carefully.
8.cn: 成功先生和失败先生都非常听话。
8.ai_notes: 'intensely': in a very strong or extreme way. 'obedient': willing to do what you are told.
8.remarks:
'intensely': 以非常强烈或极端的方式。
'obedient': 愿意听从命令。

9.b1: They react to your instructions immediately.
9.cn: 他们会立刻对你的指令做出反应。
9.ai_notes: 'snap to attention': a military phrase meaning to react or stand ready immediately.
9.remarks: 'snap to attention': 一个军事用语，意为立即做出反应或站好待命。

10.original: All you need do to signal either foreman is to give the slightest mental beck and call.
10.b1: You only need to think about them to give them a signal.
10.cn: 你只需要在心里稍微召唤一下，就能给他们发出信号。
10.ai_notes: 'beck and call': an idiom meaning to be ready to serve or obey someone instantly. 'slightest': the smallest possible amount.
10.remarks:
'beck and call': 一个习语，意为随时准备服从某人的差遣。
'slightest': 最小可能的程度。

11.original: If the signal is positive, Mr. Triumph will step forward and go to work.
11.b1: If your signal is positive, Mr. Triumph starts to work.
11.cn: 如果信号是积极的，成功先生就会站出来开始工作。
11.ai_notes: 'step forward': to come out and take responsibility or action.
11.remarks: 'step forward': 站出来承担责任或采取行动。

12.original: Likewise, a negative signal brings Mr. Defeat forward.
12.b1: If your signal is negative, Mr. Defeat starts to work.
12.cn: 同样，消极的信号会把失败先生叫出来。
12.ai_notes: 'likewise': a formal way to say 'in the same way' or 'similarly'.
12.remarks: 'likewise': 一种正式的说法，意为“同样地”或“类似地”。

13.original: To see how these two foremen work for you, try this example.
13.b1: Try this example to see how they work for you.
13.cn: 试试这个例子，看看这两位工头是如何为你工作的。
13.ai_notes: 'work for you': to be useful or beneficial to your goals.
13.remarks: 'work for you': 对你的目标有用或有益。

14.original: Tell yourself, Today is a lousy day.
14.b1: Tell yourself: 'Today is a bad day.'
14.cn: 告诉自己：“今天真是糟糕透了。”
14.ai_notes: 'lousy': a common informal word for very bad or poor quality.
14.remarks: 'lousy': 一个常用的非正式词汇，意为非常糟糕或质量很差。

15.original: This signals Mr. Defeat into action, and he manufactures some facts to prove you are right.
15.b1: This signals Mr. Defeat, and he finds facts to prove you are right.
15.cn: 这会触发失败先生开始工作，他会制造一些事实来证明你是对的。
15.ai_notes: 'into action': to start moving or working toward a goal.
15.remarks: 'into action': 开始行动或朝着目标努力。

16.original: He suggests to you that it's too hot or it's too cold.
16.b1: He tells you that it is too hot or too cold.
16.cn: 他会向你暗示天气太热或太冷。
16.ai_notes: 'suggests to you': to put an idea into your mind.
16.remarks: 'suggests to you': 将一个想法植入你的脑海。

17.b1: He says business will be bad today.
17.ai_notes: 'Business will be bad': a common way to say that sales or work will not go well.
17.remarks: 'Business will be bad': 常用的说法，意为销售或工作进展不顺。

18.b1: He says sales will go down.
18.ai_notes: 'Sales will drop': a business term meaning that the number of items sold will decrease.
18.remarks: 'Sales will drop': 商业术语，意为销售数量减少。

19.original: Other people will be on edge.
19.b1: He says other people will be angry.
19.ai_notes: 'on edge': an idiom meaning feeling nervous, irritable, or tense.
19.remarks: 'on edge': 一个习语，意为感到紧张、烦躁或焦虑。

20.b1: He says you might get sick.
20.ai_notes: 'get sick': a common phrasal verb meaning to become ill.
20.remarks: 'get sick': 一个常用的短语动词，意为生病。

21.original: Your wife will be in a fussy mood.
21.b1: He says your wife will be in a bad mood.
21.ai_notes: 'fussy mood': a state of being easily annoyed or hard to please.
21.remarks: 'fussy mood': 容易烦躁或难以取悦的状态。

22.b1: Mr. Defeat is very fast and efficient.
22.ai_notes: 'tremendously': a word used to emphasize that something is very great or extreme.
22.remarks: 'tremendously': 用于强调某事非常巨大或极端。

23.original: In just a few moments he's got you sold.
23.b1: In a few moments, he makes you believe it.
23.cn: 没过多久，他就让你相信了这一切。
23.ai_notes: 'got you sold': an idiom meaning to make someone believe or accept an idea completely.
23.remarks: 'got you sold': 一个习语，意为让某人完全相信或接受某个想法。

24.b1: You believe it is a bad day.
24.ai_notes: 'It is a bad day': a simple statement used to summarize a negative experience.
24.remarks: 'It is a bad day': 用于总结负面经历的简单陈述。

25.original: Before you know it, it is a heck of a bad day.
25.b1: Soon, you feel like it is a terrible day.
25.cn: 很快，你就会觉得这简直是糟糕透顶的一天。
25.ai_notes: 'heck of a': an informal, slightly emphatic way to say 'very' or 'a significant' (often used for negative things).
25.remarks: 'heck of a': 一种非正式且带有强调意味的说法，意为“非常”或“相当大的”（常用于负面事物）。

26.original: But tell yourself, today is a fine day, and Mr. Triumph is signaled forward to act.
26.b1: But if you say 'Today is a good day,' Mr. Triumph starts to work.
26.cn: 但如果你告诉自己“今天是个好日子”，成功先生就会被叫出来。
26.ai_notes: 'signaled forward': to be called or prompted to start an action.
26.remarks: 'signaled forward': 被呼叫或提示开始行动。

27.original: He tells you, this is a wonderful day, the weather is refreshing, it's good to be alive,
27.b1: He tells you it is a great day, the weather is nice, and it is good to be alive.
27.cn: 他会告诉你今天很棒，天气很舒服，活着真好。
27.ai_notes: 'refreshing': something that makes you feel cool, clean, or energized. 'good to be alive': an expression of gratitude for life.
27.remarks:
'refreshing': 让人感到清爽、干净或充满活力。
'good to be alive': 对生命表示感激的表达。

28.original: today you can catch up on some of your work, and then it is a good day.
28.b1: He says you can finish your work, and then it becomes a good day.
28.cn: 他会说你可以趁今天完成一些工作，于是这真的变成了一个好日子。
28.ai_notes: 'catch up on': a phrasal verb meaning to do work that you should have done earlier.
28.remarks: 'catch up on': 一个短语动词，意为补做之前应该完成的工作。

29.original: In like fashion, Mr. Defeat can show you why you can't sell Mr. Smith.
29.b1: In the same way, Mr. Defeat can show you why you cannot sell to Mr. Smith.
29.cn: 同样地，失败先生能向你展示为什么你无法说服史密斯先生购买。
29.ai_notes: 'In like fashion': a formal way to say 'in the same manner'.
29.remarks: 'In like fashion': 一种正式的说法，意为“以同样的方式”。

30.original: Mr. Triumph will show you that you can.
30.b1: Mr. Triumph will show you that you can.
30.cn: 成功先生则会向你展示你一定能做到。
30.ai_notes: 'show you that you can': to provide evidence or confidence that success is possible.
30.remarks: 'show you that you can': 提供证据或信心，证明成功是可能的。

31.original: Mr. Defeat will convince you that you will fail, while Mr. Triumph will demonstrate why you will succeed.
31.b1: Mr. Defeat convinces you that you will fail, but Mr. Triumph shows you how to succeed.
31.cn: 失败先生会让你相信你会失败，而成功先生会证明你为什么会成功。
31.ai_notes: 'convince': to make someone believe that something is true. 'demonstrate': to show clearly how something works or why it is true.
31.remarks:
'convince': 使某人相信某事是真的。
'demonstrate': 清晰地展示某事如何运作或为何正确。

32.original: Mr. Defeat will prepare a brilliant case against Tom, while Mr. Triumph will show you more reasons why you like Tom.
32.b1: Mr. Defeat finds reasons to dislike Tom, but Mr. Triumph shows you why you like him.
32.cn: 失败先生会准备好讨厌汤姆的理由，而成功先生会让你看到更多喜欢汤姆的理由。
32.ai_notes: 'brilliant case': a very strong or clever argument for a specific point of view.
32.remarks: 'brilliant case': 针对特定观点非常有说服力或巧妙的论证。

33.original: Now, the more work you give either of these two foremen, the stronger he becomes.
33.b1: The more work you give to one of them, the stronger he becomes.
33.cn: 你给这两位工头的工作越多，他们就变得越强大。
33.ai_notes: 'the more... the stronger': a grammatical structure used to show how two things change together.
33.remarks: 'the more... the stronger': 一种语法结构，用于展示两件事如何同步变化。

34.original: If Mr. Defeat is given more work to do, he adds personnel and takes up more space in your mind.
34.b1: If you give Mr. Defeat more work, he hires more people and takes up more space in your mind.
34.cn: 如果你给失败先生更多工作，他就会增加人手，占据你大脑中更多的空间。
34.ai_notes: 'adds personnel': to hire or bring in more people to help with work.
34.remarks: 'adds personnel': 雇佣或引入更多人手来协助工作。

35.original: Eventually, he will take over the entire thought-manufacturing division and virtually all thought will be of a negative nature.
35.b1: Eventually, he controls all your thoughts, and they all become negative.
35.cn: 最终，他会接管整个思想制造部门，你所有的想法都会变得消极。
35.ai_notes: 'take over': to gain control of something. 'virtually': almost entirely.
35.remarks:
'take over': 接管某事。
'virtually': 几乎完全地。

36.original: The only wise thing to do is fire Mr. Defeat.
36.b1: The best thing to do is to fire Mr. Defeat.
36.cn: 最明智的做法就是解雇失败先生。
36.remarks: 'wise': 拥有或表现出良好的判断力。

37.ai_notes: 'You don't need him': a direct way to say that something is unnecessary.
37.remarks: 'You don't need him': 直接表达某物是不必要的。

38.original: You don't want him around telling you that you can't, you're not up to it,
38.b1: You do not want him telling you that you cannot do things or that you are not good enough.
38.cn: 你不需要他在你耳边说你做不到、你不行。
38.ai_notes: 'not up to it': an idiom meaning you lack the ability or strength to do something.
38.remarks: 'not up to it': 一个习语，意为缺乏能力或力量去做某事。

39.b1: You do not want him saying you will fail.
39.cn: 你也不需要他告诉你你会失败等等。
39.ai_notes: 'and so on': a phrase used to indicate that there are more similar things you could mention.
39.remarks: 'and so on': 用于表示还有更多类似的事情可以提及。

40.original: Mr. Defeat won't help you get where you want to go, so boot him out.
40.b1: Mr. Defeat will not help you reach your goals, so get rid of him.
40.cn: 失败先生帮不了你实现目标，所以把他踢走吧。
40.ai_notes: 'boot him out': an informal, forceful way to say 'remove' or 'fire' someone.
40.remarks: 'boot him out': 一种非正式且强硬的说法，意为“开除”或“赶走”某人。

41.original: Use Mr. Triumph 100% of the time.
41.b1: Use Mr. Triumph all the time.
41.cn: 百分之百地使用成功先生。
41.ai_notes: '100% of the time': an expression meaning always or constantly.
41.remarks: '100% of the time': 意为总是或持续不断。

42.original: When any thought enters your mind, ask Mr. Triumph to go to work for you.
42.b1: When you have a thought, ask Mr. Triumph to work for you.
42.cn: 当任何想法进入你的脑海时，请让成功先生为你工作。
42.ai_notes: 'enters your mind': a common way to say you have a new thought.
42.remarks: 'enters your mind': 产生新想法的常用说法。

43.original: He'll show you how you can succeed.
43.b1: He will show you how to succeed.
43.cn: 他会向你展示你如何才能成功。
43.ai_notes: 'show you how': to provide a path or method for achieving something.
43.remarks: 'show you how': 提供实现目标的路径或方法。
